element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used throughout but has risk against final boss (depletes health or fully heals enemy if they've taken damage)
Player gains new Ninja Arts after each boss defeat; can select which to use via Start menu
Required as first attack against final boss in specific sequence
Required as second attack against final boss; activated differently between regions
Required as third attack against final boss; enables tactical repositioning (going behind enemy)
Required as final attack against final boss El Toro
Non-mandatory collectibles; narrative framework but no gameplay bonus
Blue mobster enemies can create cheap damage scenarios (removed from European version in Stage 1); enemy types progress through levels
Each level boss requires mastery of specific mechanics; final boss unique in requiring sequential Ninja Art usage
Perspective shift near end of final stage; represents spatial and narrative climax
Japanese version: all five previous bosses fought before final boss (difficulty spike); International version: only one previous boss

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final Boss fight (El Toro holds Taro)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unique constraint: collision with El Toro = instant death; shuriken attacks risky; must use specific Ninja Arts in order

typical_relief_triggers: Learning new Ninja Art after boss defeat
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy ambushes (e.g., blue mobster dropping on barrels)
Final level perspective shift and difficulty escalation
Final boss mechanic: must use four Ninja Arts in exact sequence or risk healing enemy
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty through level progression with strategic pauses at level transitions; final stage compresses tension with boss rematches and perspective shift; final boss creates unique tension through mandatory ability sequencing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden kidnapped children scattered throughout each level (non-mandatory rescue)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic combat and first Ninja Art; manageable enemy encounters; blue mobster cheap shot mechanic exists in Japanese/US versions
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy variety and complexity; new Ninja Arts unlocked after each level providing strategic options; boss encounters require adaptation to individual patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final level introduces first-person perspective segments; Japanese version forces all five previous bosses as rematches before final boss; international version simplified to one rematch
notable_spikes: Final boss El Toro: requires sequential use of four Ninja Arts in correct order; collision = instant death; shuriken attacks are high-risk; projectile evasion under hostage constraint
Japanese version final stage: 6 floors with all five previous bosses must be defeated before El Toro
International version final stage: reduced to 4 floors with single boss rematch before El Toro (difficulty reduction)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level culminates in a boss encounter that gates progression and unlocks new Ninja Art. Boss design often requires specific approach (e.g., Level 1 boss can only be hit from high jumps). Defeating boss triggers narrative cutscene and Ninja Art learning.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels 1-4; varies by region (Level 4 is Electric Ghost in Japanese version, Voodoo Warrior in international)

notable_level_patterns.description: Kidnapped children placed throughout levels as optional objectives. Non-critical to progression but serves narrative framing. Each rescued child has unique appearance per stage (except final 2 stages which share graphics).
Omitted stage between Level 1-2 in Japanese version themed around climbing between buildings with mobsters firing from windows. Removed from international releases.
Final level partially viewed through first-person perspective toward stage end, creating spatial and sensory shift before boss encounters. Emphasizes narrative intensity.

Japanese version: player fights all five previous bosses sequentially before final boss. International version: reduced to single previous boss rematch. Creates resource attrition and pattern-recognition climax.

El Toro fight unique: player must defeat boss using four Ninja Arts in mandatory sequence (Fire Bomb → Fire Ring → Spider → Missiles). Collision = instant death. Shuriken attacks risk full enemy heal if boss damaged. Hostage constraint (Taro) adds narrative tension.
